models.name: personrelation_sumtotal
models.description: This table captures the relationships between individuals and their respective managers within the organization, providing a comprehensive view of reporting structures. Use this table when you need to: 1. Generate manager-specific learning reports and dashboards, 2. Implement manager approval workflows for learning requests, 3. Create hierarchical learning compliance reports, 4. Track learning progress across management levels, 5. Enable manager notifications for team learning activities, 6. Implement cascading learning requirements through management hierarchy, 7. Create organizational learning analytics by management level, 8. Support succession planning and leadership development programs.

properties.description: Foreign key reference to a person in the database, linking to the person entity.

This column establishes the employee's direct reporting line by linking to the record of their primary manager. As a self-referencing foreign key, it contains the identifier of another person within this same person_sumtotal table.
Foreign key reference to the second manager in the person relation hierarchy.
Foreign key referencing the third-level manager in the person relation hierarchy.
Foreign key reference to the fourth manager in the person relation hierarchy.
Foreign key reference to the fifth-level manager in the person relation hierarchy.
Foreign key reference to the sixth level manager in the person relation hierarchy.
Foreign key reference to the manager associated with a person in the personrelation_sumtotal table.
Foreign key reference to the eighth manager in the person relation table.
Foreign key reference to the ninth manager in the person relation hierarchy.
Timestamp indicating when the record was created, stored as a string.
This table captures the relationships between individuals and their respective managers within the organization, providing a comprehensive view of reporting structures.
queryPatterns: Identify direct manager for a person (manager1fk)
Build organizational reporting hierarchies (manager1fk, manager2fk, etc.)
Find persons managed by a specific manager
Analyze manager-employee relationships by creation or modification date
useCases: Organizational Hierarchy Mapping: Define and visualize reporting structures within the company
Managerial Reporting: Generate reports on team learning progress and compliance for managers
Workflow Automation: Implement manager approval workflows for learning requests
Succession Planning: Identify potential leaders and track their development paths
HR Analytics: Analyze span of control and management effectiveness metrics
